kamman­.meaning: work and arrangement (first part of kammantasaṁvidhānena, "by the arrangement of work")
kamman­.summary: This is a line-break fragment: kamman-tasaṁvidhānena is the single compound kammantasaṁvidhānena split across lines in the source.
kamman­.gram: fragment of a neuter compound noun (kammanta + saṁvidhāna, instrumental singular)
kamman­.note: uncertain: the headword is a hyphenation fragment from the source text; see kammantasaṁvidhānena for the full word.

kammapaṭisaraṇo.meaning: having kamma as one's refuge; kamma as one's reconciliation
kammapaṭisaraṇo.summary: From the reflection on kamma: "kamma is my reconciliation (refuge)".

kammaseṭṭhassa.meaning: of the doer of the highest work

kammasocanaṁ.meaning: sorrowing over one's action; bewailing a deed

kammassakatāñāṇaṁ.meaning: the knowledge that kamma is one's own possession
breakdown: The state of being one's own. (The doubling ss is junction doubling.)

kammassako.meaning: having kamma as one's own possession
kammassako.summary: The opening term of the reflection "kamma is my only possession"; its opposite assaka/appassaka means having little or nothing of one's own.
breakdown: One's own. (The doubling ss is junction doubling.)

kammassakomhi.meaning: I am the owner of my kamma; kamma is my own possession
kammassakomhi.gram: idiom: adjective + verb (kammassako + amhi "I am", fused by sandhi)
breakdown: Having kamma as one's own possession.
I am (1st person singular of atthi). (The final -o fuses with a-: kammassako + amhi → kammassakomhi.)

kammavaṭṭavipākavaṭṭavasena.meaning: on account of the round of kamma and the round of its results
breakdown: On account of, by way of (instrumental of vasa).

kammavisuddhimattano.meaning: the purity of one's own deeds
kammavisuddhimattano.gram: sandhi fusion: noun + noun + genitive pronoun (kamma + visuddhiṁ + attano)
breakdown: Of oneself, one's own (genitive of attā).

kammayoni.meaning: born from one's kamma; having kamma as one's womb
kammayoni.summary: From the reflection on kamma: "I am born from my kamma".

kammaṭṭhānena.meaning: by means of action; through the base of action
breakdown: Place, base, means. (The doubling ṭṭh is junction doubling.)

kammāsampī.meaning: even the blemished one; though variegated, stained
kammāsampī.gram: adjective (kammāsa) + enclitic particle api, fused by sandhi
breakdown: Even, also. (Fused: kammāsam + api → kammāsampī with the vowel lengthened in verse.)

kantā.gram: past participle of kāmeti (√kam), here feminine/plural form
breakdown: Past participle suffix: loved, hence pleasing, charming.

kappa.meaning: an aeon; a fixed period of individual or cosmic life
kappa.summary: A simile compares a kappa to a period not exhausted even if someone removed one seed every hundred years from a granary one yojana (about seven miles) in length, breadth and height, filled with tiny mustard seeds.
breakdown: Root: to be fit, to arrange (kappati).
Noun suffix; kappa is literally a fitting or fixed ordering, hence a fixed span of time.

kappati.meaning: to be fit, proper, suitable; to be allowable

kappeti.meaning: to make; to carry on; to arrange
kappeti.gram: verb, 3rd person singular present, causative of kappati
breakdown: Root: to be fit, to arrange.

karaṇaṁ.meaning: doing; making; the act or instrument of doing
karaṇaṁ.gram: neuter action noun from karoti (nominative/accusative singular)
karaṇaṁ.note: uncertain: the lexicon's gloss line for this row is garbled (it runs together with a neighbouring row about "restrained + in consequence of"); the meaning given follows the standard sense supported by the DPD analysis.

karaṇīya.meaning: something that should be done; a duty
breakdown: Potential participle suffix: "that ought to be done".

karaṇīyametta suttaṁ.meaning: the Karaṇīyamettasutta, the Discourse on What Ought to Be Done (the Mettā Sutta)
karaṇīyametta suttaṁ.gram: phrase: proper noun (title of a sutta)

karaṇīyaṁ.gram: potential participle of karoti, neuter nominative/accusative singular
breakdown: Potential participle suffix: "that ought to be done".

karaṇīyo.meaning: what ought to be done; duty, obligation
karaṇīyo.gram: potential participle of karoti, masculine nominative singular
breakdown: Potential participle suffix: "that ought to be done".

karissāmi.meaning: I will perform; I will do
karissāmi.gram: verb, 1st person singular future of karoti

karontenapi.meaning: even while doing; and by one doing
karontenapi.gram: present participle of karoti (instrumental singular) + enclitic particle pi

karotha.meaning: you should make; do! (addressed to several people)
karotha.gram: verb, 2nd person plural imperative of karoti

kasigorakkhe.meaning: farming and cattle-keeping; farmer and cowherd

kasiravuttike.meaning: finding it hard to support oneself; of difficult livelihood

kasirena.gram: adjective kasira in the instrumental singular, used adverbially

kasmā.meaning: why?; from what?; of what sort?
kasmā.gram: interrogative pronoun, ablative singular of ka/ko ("who, what")

kasāmivā.gram: noun + comparison particle (kasaṁ + iva, fused in verse)
breakdown: Like, as (comparison particle; vowel lengthened in verse).

katabhāvajānanañāṇaṁ.meaning: the knowledge that understands what development has been done

katakaraṇīyo.meaning: one who has done what ought to be done
katakaraṇīyo.summary: A stock description of the Arahant: the task is fulfilled.
breakdown: What ought to be done (potential participle of karoti).

katakaraṇīyā.meaning: those who have done what ought to be done
breakdown: What ought to be done (potential participle of karoti).

katapurebhattakicco.meaning: having fulfilled the duties of the morning (before the meal)

katapuññatā.meaning: the merit of past good deeds; having done meritorious deeds

katapuñño.meaning: one who has done meritorious deeds

katavedī.meaning: grateful; literally, understanding what has been done for oneself
breakdown: What has been done (past participle of karoti).
What has been done (past participle of karoti).

kataññū.meaning: grateful; literally, knowing what has been done for oneself
breakdown: What has been done (past participle of karoti).
Knowing (from jānāti; kata + ñū with junction doubling).

kathaṁ.note: uncertain: the lexicon's gloss line for this row is garbled (it runs together with the neighbouring kappa and kappati rows); the meaning given is the standard interrogative sense "how?".

kathāvatthu.meaning: the Kathāvatthu ("Points of Controversy"), a book of the Abhidhamma; subject of talk
kathāvatthu.gram: neuter noun, compound; also a proper noun (title)

kattabbakiccajānanañāṇaṁ.meaning: the knowledge that understands the duty that should be done
breakdown: That should be done (potential participle of karoti).

kattabbaṁ.gram: potential participle of karoti (√kar + -tabba), neuter nominative/accusative singular
breakdown: Potential participle suffix: "that should be done".

kattikapavāraṇadivase.meaning: on the day of the Pavāraṇā ceremony in the month of Kattika
breakdown: The month of Kattika (October–November), the close of the rains retreat.
The Pavāraṇā, the ceremony of mutual invitation that ends the rains retreat.

kattukāmo.meaning: desirous of doing; wanting to perform
kattukāmo.gram: adjective, compound (infinitive kattuṁ + kāma; masculine nominative singular)
